title: PxDocument
description: A complete .px document — the root AST node.

Accessor.description: Access path segment on a variable or expression.

ConfigDecl.description: ```px config defaults: max_retries: 3 timeout: 5000 nested: key: "value" ```

ConstraintDecl.description: ```px constraint no_empty_responses: scope: response phase: pre_send require: response.length > 0 severity: error message: "Empty responses are never acceptable" ```

ContractDecl.description: ```px contract tone_matching: given: "User sends casual message" when: "Responding to user" then: "Match casual tone" threshold: 0.8 examples: - input: "hey what's up" expect: "casual_tone" ```

DataflowParam.description: A typed parameter with optional queue binding. `name: Type from "queue_name"`

source_queue.description: The queue this parameter reads from.

DataflowProcedureDecl.description: ```px procedure classify_and_route(msg: InboundMessage from "inbound") -> RouteDecision into "route_decision": given: "Classify message intent and determine routing" classify_intent msg.content -> $intent route_by_intent $intent -> $destination return {intent: $intent, destination: $destination} ```

DataflowReturn.description: Return type with optional queue binding. `-> Type into "queue_name"`

dest_queue.description: The queue output is pushed to.

EntityDecl.description: ```px entity Player: prefix: "player" fields: health: int name: string inventory: list[string] ```

FactDecl.description: ```px fact MemoryEntry: content: string category: string timestamp: int ```

FieldDecl.description: A typed field declaration used in entities, facts, and function params.

FunctionDecl.description: ```px function classify_intent(message: string) -> string: mode: deterministic """Classify the user's intent from message content.""" ```

Ident.description: An identifier (variable name, construct name, field name). Invariant: ASCII_ALPHA | "_" followed by ASCII_ALPHANUMERIC | "_"

LegacyProcedureDecl.description: ```px procedure handle_message: trigger: on_write("inbound") params: [$message] given: "Process an incoming message" steps: classify_intent $message -> $intent ... ```

oneOf.description: v2: Rust-style code block with braces

ProcedureTrigger.description: Legacy trigger (deprecated — prefer dataflow queue bindings).

RuleDecl.description: ```px rule detect_urgency: priority: 10 when: - message.contains("urgent") then: - action: flag_priority level: "high" ```

ScenarioDecl.description: ```px scenario test_routing: given: "User sends a question" setup: define $msg = "What time is it?" run: classify_intent {input: $msg} expect: - result_contains {text: "question"} ```

Statement.description:
A top-level statement in a .px file.

Adjacently tagged (`{ "kind": "Entity", "value": { .. } }`) so the JSON projection is stable and legible for TS/schema consumers and handles every variant shape uniformly (PXLANG-M2 §3.1.2).

StringLiteral.description: A string literal (single or double quoted in source). The value is the content WITHOUT surrounding quotes.

TriggerDecl.description: ```px trigger daily_consolidation: on: timer schedule: "0 3 * * *" run: consolidate_memories ```

TypeExpr.description: A type expression used in field declarations, params, and return types.

oneOf.description: User-defined type name (refers to an entity or fact)

Value.description: A value that can appear in declarations, step arguments, or config entries.

oneOf.description: Arithmetic expression: `a + b` (simple binary in value position)
Bare identifier used as value (e.g., enum variant name)
